Review 3 (CHM2210, 2026-05-05):
Avoid Lawrence because he is dismissive and overcomplicates everything. I drowned him out and used the textbook since it is a lifesaver and the only reason I learned. TAs are not helpful at all and he forces rushed bubble sheets with no time to think. Expect to wait weeks for grades while being left to drown by a prof with zero empathy.
Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy

Review 4 (CHM2210, 2026-05-05):
Yes he is sassy and blunt, but it is a hard class. He is always there for you with questions and even though he is sassy, he makes sure you understand the content. The flipped classroom style really helped me understand better and we do a lot of in class work that he walks us through and explains super well. I wouldn't do chem with anyone else.

Dr. Lawrence is a very hard professor to take IF you struggle with structure in your own time. I took him for Orgo 1 & 2, and he was great, and super nice during office hours. I personally struggle with the style of teaching he does (flipped classroom, barely hw, no study guides), but his teaching prepares you if planning on going to grad school.

Review 1 (COP2500C, 2026-06-06):
She's very friendly and explains things slowly (sometimes too slowly) for students. The class is very easy so long as you do the work, tests are accurate to what is taught as well. I hardly attended lectures but had no problem using the resources she provides to catch up. Great teacher and format. Grading is fair.

School: University of Central Florida
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1583836

Review 2 (COP2500C, 2026-05-07):
I personally did not enjoy professor Angell's class. She goes through the material very fast and it is hard to keep up. She also doesn't explain some concepts thoroughly. The midterm and final can each be taken twice so that is fine overall. The homework is hard and a simple extra letter can get you 25 points deducted, so that is a downside.
Tags: (mixed)

Review 3 (COP2500C, 2026-05-06):
I LOVED THIS CLASS. She made coding so enjoyable for me that I changed my major to Computer Engineering. She is an absolutely amazing professor. The class is entirely just lectures with weekly assignments and labs. Nothing hard at all. Two attempts on the one midterm and final. But you probably won't even need it. Super easy and super fun. Enjoy!!!
